summary_en: New medium-term plan (FY3/27–FY3/29) announced. Target: stepwise ROE improvement through segment-specific growth strategies, disciplined balance-sheet management, and capital-return reinforcement. Capital cost and stock-price awareness update follows TSE requirement; prior February 2025 disclosure updated.
title_en: Announcement of New Medium-Term Management Plan and Update on Initiatives toward the Realization of Management Conscious of Capital Cost and Stock Price
translation_en_brief:
On 15 May 2026, Nihon Yamamura Glass announced a new three-year medium-term management plan covering fiscal years ending March 2027 through March 2029, labeled "Phase 2: Leap toward Sustained Growth." The company positions this plan as a successor to the prior period (FY3/24–FY3/26), during which it completed business restructuring and achieved significant earnings improvement while strengthening shareholder returns and pursuing ROE enhancement.

Under the new plan, the company will execute segment-specific business strategies to drive growth and earnings expansion across divisions, while implementing disciplined balance-sheet management with conscious attention to capital productivity. The stated objective is to achieve steady and stepwise ROE improvement.

Concurrently, in response to Tokyo Stock Exchange guidance on "management conscious of capital cost and stock price," the company has conducted a current-state analysis and evaluation, updating its prior disclosure published in February 2025. Full details are provided in the attached materials.
